title: Credit Card Delinquencies Test Multi-Year Highs As Job Market Faces 'Material' Worsening, Capital One Warns
text: There are "nascent signs of trouble brewing" in the world of consumer credit card debt, one analyst cautions.

title: Capital One (COF) Q1 Earnings Lag as Provisions Rise, Stock Down
text: An increase in credit costs and non-interest expenses and lower non-interest income hurt Capital One's (COF) Q1 earnings while higher NII offers some support.

title: Capital One Financial Corporation (COF) Q1 2023 Earnings Call Transcript

title: Capital One Reports Quarterly Lending Decreases Across the Board
text: Capital One Financial's latest results show that, like consumers, it isn't immune to the market. “We are expecting inflation to impact consumer credit by depressing real incomes, as well from an unemployment effect.

title: Capital One (COF) Q1 Earnings and Revenues Lag Estimates
text: Capital One (COF) came out with quarterly earnings of $2.31 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $3.80 per share. This compares to earnings of $5.62 per share a year ago.

title: Capital One's deposits rise, but quarterly results are a miss
text: Capital One Financial Corp. stock dropped more than 4% in the extended session Thursday after the financial company missed Wall Street expectations for its first quarter despite a rise in retail deposits. Capital One earned $960 million, or $2.31 a share, in the first quarter, compared with $2.4 billion, or $5.62 a share, in the first quarter of 2022.

title: 5 Undervalued Stocks in Keynote Speaker Bernard Horn's Portfolio
text: As investors gear up for the 2023 GuruFocus Value Conference, which takes place in two weeks, five stocks in keynote speaker Bernard Horn (Trades, Portfolio)'s fourth-quarter equity portfolio that are undervalued based on the discounted cash flow model are Colony Bankcorp Inc. ( CBAN , Financial), Berry Global Group Inc. ( BERY , Financial), Capital One Financial Corp. ( COF , Financial), Tyson Foods Inc. ( TSN , Financial) and Allison Transmission Holdings Inc. ( ALSN , Financial).

title: What Should Investors Watch in Regional Bank Earnings?
text: We're about to see how the recent banking turmoil affected regional financial institutions.

title: Walmart Tensions Reveal Cracks in Capital One's Business
text: Capital One Financial Corp. ( COF , Financial) has been the exclusive issuer for Walmart ( WMT , Financial) credit cards in the U.S. since 2018. However, last week, the discount retailer filed a lawsuit to end the partnership early, with the official argument that Capital One was not living up to the customer service standards stipulated in the contract.

title: Tupperware, Micron Technology, Capital One and other big movers ahead of Monday's stock market open
text: These were the biggest moving stocks ahead of Monday's open. Gainers: Shares of Micron Technology Inc. MU, +2.91% climbed 5.6% in premarket trading, getting a boost after production cuts announced by rival Samsung Electronics Co. Ltd 005930, +1.08%.

title: Capital One Financial falls in premarket after Walmart suit report
text: Capital One Financial COF, +0.09% fell 3.5% in premarket trades Monday after reports that Walmart Inc. WMT, +0.75% sued the credit-card giant in an effort to scrap a credit-card partnership. The Wall Street Journal reported that Walmart has filed its suit because Capital One allegedly came up short on its service obligations such as failing to replace lost cards or post some transactions to customers' accounts in a timely manner.

title: Walmart Suit Seeks to End Capital One Card Relationship
text: Walmart has reportedly sued Capital One to end a 4-year-old credit card partnership. According to published reports Friday (April 7), the retail giant accuses the bank of failing to live up to its customer care standards.

title: Walmart sues Capital One in effort to end credit-card partnership: WSJ
text: Big-box retailer Walmart Inc. WMT this week sued credit-card giant Capital One Financial Corp. COF in an effort to scrap a credit-card partnership between the two, according to The Wall Street Journal. Walmart's lawsuit alleged that Capital One came up short on its service obligations, failing to replace lost cards or post some transactions to customers' accounts in a timely manner — claims the credit-card provider said were “immaterial,” the Journal said.

title: Walmart Sues Credit-Card Partner Capital One
text: The retailer is seeking to end the two companies' credit-card partnership early.

title: Why Capital One Stock Plummeted 11.8% in March
text: Capital One is the ninth-largest bank in the country and one of the leading credit card issuers. The company reports first-quarter earnings on April 27.

title: Grab Buffett-Style Opportunities: 2 Fat Yields Up To 9%
text: Warren Buffett has always been greedy when investors are fearful. Mr. Buffett's dealmaking during troubled times often involves a hefty "waiting fee."
